wines.name: Clos Sainte-Hune
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; long lees ageing in tank, no oak

taste.summary: Trimbach's 1.67 hectare monopole inside Grand Cru Rosacker, declassified to Alsace AOC by family preference. Piercing dry Riesling that needs a decade to unwind into wax, citrus and Vosges granite salinity.

pairings.why: Riesling's racy acid cuts the lard and brine of slow-cooked sauerkraut and sausage.
Iodine and saline lift the bivalves and crustaceans of an Alsace seafood platter.
Vosges washed-rind cow's milk needs Riesling's acid to scrub the pungent cream.

history.summary: Bottled as a single-vineyard cuvee since 1919, Clos Sainte-Hune is a walled parcel inside Grand Cru Rosacker that Trimbach has historically labelled Alsace AOC rather than Grand Cru, citing dissatisfaction with the wider Rosacker delimitation.

milestones.event: First single-vineyard Clos Sainte-Hune bottling
Rosacker promoted to Grand Cru; Trimbach declines to use the Grand Cru mention
Hubert Trimbach reaffirms the Alsace AOC labelling in trade interviews

wines.description: Trimbach's 1.67 hectare walled monopole inside Grand Cru Rosacker, family-declassified to Alsace AOC. The benchmark for dry, ageworthy Alsace Riesling.

wines.name: Cuvee Frederic Emile Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; extended ageing in tank then cellar age before release

taste.summary: Trimbach's flagship Riesling blends Grand Cru Geisberg and Osterberg fruit. Dry, structured, smoky, built to age but rich enough to drink young.

pairings.why: The wine echoes the very Riesling used in the Alsace braise; matched weight and savoury fruit.
Smoky minerality lifts the cream-and-butter sauce on the freshwater fish.
Riesling salinity and crystalline cheese share the same brothy umami.

history.summary: Named for the Trimbach forebear who reorganised the maison around Riesling in the 19th century, Cuvee Frederic Emile sources from Geisberg and Osterberg Grand Cru parcels around Ribeauville and is released after several years of bottle age.

milestones.event: First bottling of Cuvee Frederic Emile
Geisberg and Osterberg promoted to Grand Cru status
Trimbach acquires additional Geisberg parcels reinforcing the blend

wines.description: Trimbach's flagship cuvee, a Riesling blend of Grand Cru Geisberg and Osterberg parcels released after several years of bottle age. Smoky, structured and ageworthy.

wines.name: Trimbach Gewurztraminer Vendanges Tardives
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; no oak

taste.summary: Late-harvested Gewurztraminer at high natural ripeness, produced only in warm vintages. Honeyed apricot and lychee with the trademark rose-petal lift.

pairings.why: Sweet exotic fruit cradles the duck liver's richness; classic Alsace pairing.
Cumin-spiced washed-rind cheese finds an echo in the wine's candied ginger.
Yellow plum tart matches the wine's stone-fruit and apricot register.

history.summary: Vendanges Tardives was codified in 1984 after a Hugel-led reform; Trimbach has made the late-harvest Gewurztraminer only in warm vintages since, often skipping cooler years.

milestones.event: Vendanges Tardives recognised in Alsace AOC decree
Trimbach declares a benchmark VT vintage
Recent warm-vintage bottling reaffirms the cuvee's selective production

wines.description: Late-harvest Gewurztraminer from Trimbach, produced only in warm Alsace vintages. Honeyed apricot, candied ginger and the rose-petal lift of botrytis-touched fruit.

wines.name: Trimbach Pinot Noir Reserve Personnelle F
wines.oak_regime: 12 to 18 months in French oak, modest new-oak share

taste.summary: Trimbach's serious Pinot Noir from old-vine Mambourg-area sources, made in a Burgundian register since the warm vintages of the late 2000s opened ripening windows in Alsace.

pairings.why: Wild cherry fruit and game register meet directly in the glass.
The lightness of Alsace Pinot suits the bacon and creme-fraiche flatbread.
Forest-floor and clove notes echo the dish's earthy register.

history.summary: The serious-Pinot-Noir Reserve Personnelle bottling marked Trimbach's commitment to taking the Alsace red grape seriously, taking advantage of warmer vintages and old plots near Mambourg and Kientzheim.

milestones.event: First Reserve Personnelle Pinot Noir release
Single-vineyard parcel selection refined for the cuvee

wines.description: Trimbach's serious Pinot Noir from old-vine Alsace plots near Mambourg, made in a Burgundian register and barrel-aged. Sour cherry, violet and fine spice.

wines.name: Trimbach Riesling Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; no oak

taste.summary: Trimbach's house-style Riesling, blending fruit from multiple villages around Ribeauville. Bone-dry, citrus and stone, the textbook introduction to Alsace Riesling.

pairings.why: Riesling acidity cuts pork brine and lard in Alsace's signature kraut platter.

pairings.dish: Poached white fish with beurre blanc
pairings.why: Crisp citrus mirrors the lemon in beurre blanc.
Saline lift and bracing acid for raw shellfish.

history.summary: The Trimbach Riesling Reserve has long served as the maison's entry-tier dry Riesling, blending vineyard parcels across Ribeauville and surrounding villages and bottled unoaked for citrus and stone immediacy.

milestones.event: Riesling Reserve established as the house-style Riesling
Sourcing widened to include additional Bas-Rhin parcels

wines.description: Trimbach's house-style dry Riesling blending parcels around Ribeauville. Bone-dry, citrus and stone; the textbook introduction to Alsace Riesling.

wines.name: Hugel Schoelhammer Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old oak foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: A single 0.45 hectare parcel high on Grand Cru Schoenenbourg, declassified to Alsace AOC because Hugel disagrees with the wider GC delimitation. Hugel's flagship dry Riesling, released as a multi-decade vintage statement.

pairings.why: Citrus and limestone slip alongside the brown-butter fish.
Saline minerality and acid carry a seafood-version Alsace choucroute.
Crystalline aged cheese meets the wine's smoky stone register.

history.summary: Schoelhammer is a single 0.45 hectare parcel within Grand Cru Schoenenbourg that Hugel has bottled as a flagship since 2007. Released as an Alsace AOC because Hugel rejects the wider Schoenenbourg GC delimitation.

milestones.event: First Schoelhammer vintage bottled and released on a slow rollout
Schoelhammer 2007 released after seven years on lees and in bottle

wines.description: Hugel's flagship single-parcel Schoenenbourg Riesling, declassified to Alsace AOC. Yellow plum, preserved lemon and limestone built for a 20-year cellar.

wines.name: Hugel Grossi Laue Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Grossi Laue (Alsatian for 'great growth') is Hugel's bottling from their oldest plots in Schoenenbourg and Pflostig. Dry, stony, structured, built to age.

pairings.why: Cream and crayfish carry the wine's smoky stone and citrus.
Salty pretzel and crisp acid is the simplest Alsace bar pairing.
Crystal-saline cheese loves the wine's mineral lift.

history.summary: Grossi Laue replaced Hugel's earlier 'Jubilee' tier from the 2007 vintage as the family's top dry range across Riesling, Pinot Gris and Gewurztraminer, sourcing exclusively from old-vine plots in Schoenenbourg and Pflostig.

milestones.event: Grossi Laue tier introduced as the family's top dry range
Grossi Laue 2007 Riesling released into market

wines.description: Hugel's top dry Riesling tier, sourced from old plots in Schoenenbourg and Pflostig. Yellow apple, smoky stone and the firm acidity of a cellar wine.

wines.name: Hugel Selection de Grains Nobles Gewurztraminer
wines.classification: Alsace AOC Selection de Grains Nobles
wines.oak_regime: Old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Botrytised Gewurztraminer at near-Sauternes ripeness, made only in noble-rot vintages. Saffron honey, candied rose and an apricot-jam palate.

pairings.why: Honeyed unctuousness pairs with the duck-liver classic.
Sweet wine balances the salty blue.
Caramelised apple meets the wine's candied stone-fruit register.

history.summary: Hugel led the modern revival of Selection de Grains Nobles, lobbying for the 1984 Alsace AOC reform that codified the category, and has bottled SGN Gewurztraminer only in noble-rot vintages.

milestones.event: Hugel revives botrytised Gewurztraminer at SGN level
SGN codified in Alsace AOC decree thanks to Hugel-led reform
Benchmark SGN vintage acclaimed by international critics

wines.description: Hugel's botrytised Gewurztraminer at SGN ripeness, made only in noble-rot vintages. Saffron honey, candied rose, apricot jam; a multi-decade dessert wine.

wines.name: Hugel Estate Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old foudre

taste.summary: Estate-only Riesling from the family's holdings in Riquewihr and Zellenberg, bottled dry and unoaked. Hugel's mid-tier Riesling.

pairings.why: Cream-and-bacon flatbread cut by Riesling's bright acid.
Crisp white fish takes the Riesling's citrus and stone register.
Saline acidity matches raw fish without overpowering it.

history.summary: Hugel restructured their range in 2013 into Estate, Grossi Laue and the Schoelhammer pinnacle. Estate Riesling sources entirely from the family's own holdings rather than buying in fruit.

milestones.event: Range reorganised; Estate tier introduced for family-owned fruit

wines.description: Hugel's estate-only Riesling from family holdings in Riquewihr and Zellenberg. Dry, citrus and stone; the maison's reliable Riesling expression.

wines.name: Hugel Gentil
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; no oak

taste.summary: Hugel's revival of the historic 'gentil' blend, with each variety vinified separately before being blended. Approachable apple, peach and a Gewurztraminer-led floral lift.

pairings.why: Aromatic blend handles a range of cured meats and pickles.
Cream-bacon flatbread softened by the wine's gentle aromatic lift.
Aromatic blend with off-dry texture handles chilli and lemongrass.

history.summary: Hugel reintroduced the historic 'gentil' bottling in 1992, restoring an old Alsace tradition of blending the four noble grapes plus Sylvaner under a single label. Each variety is fermented separately before blending.

milestones.event: Hugel revives the historic gentil blend
Gentil becomes Hugel's largest-volume export

wines.description: Hugel's revival of the historic Alsace gentil blend: noble grapes vinified separately then blended. Aromatic apple, peach and a Gewurztraminer-led lift.

wines.name: Hugel Pinot Noir Les Neveux
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, modest new-oak share

taste.summary: Hugel's serious Pinot Noir, named for the cousins of the 13th-generation family, sourced from old plots around Riquewihr and Hunawihr. Burgundian register with Alsace lift.

pairings.why: Pinot's red-fruit and silk pairs with creamy Alsace chicken-in-wine.
Cherry fruit and fine tannin handle the bird's dark meat.
Forest-floor undercurrent meets the rice and porcini.

history.summary: Les Neveux is Hugel's serious Pinot Noir, introduced from the 2017 vintage as a tribute to the cousins of the 13th-generation family. Built from old plots around Riquewihr.

milestones.event: First vintage of Les Neveux Pinot Noir released

wines.description: Hugel's serious Pinot Noir from old plots around Riquewihr, barrel-aged. Burgundian-register fruit, fine spice and silky tannin from the 13th generation.

wines.name: Leon Beyer Cuvee des Comtes d Eguisheim Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Beyer's flagship Riesling, sourced from old vines on the Pfersigberg slopes around Eguisheim. Steely, dry, mineral; built for a long cellar.

pairings.why: Lobster bisque's iodine and shellfish meets the wine's saline drive.
Almond, brown butter and freshwater fish slip with Riesling minerality.
Champagne-region kraut cuts well against Beyer's dry mineral Riesling.

history.summary: Beyer rejects the Grand Cru system on philosophical grounds. The Cuvee des Comtes d Eguisheim Riesling, sourced from old plots on the Pfersigberg slopes, has been the maison's flagship Alsace AOC Riesling since 1971.

milestones.event: First Comtes d Eguisheim Riesling bottling
Beyer publicly declines to use the Grand Cru mention on Pfersigberg fruit

wines.description: Beyer's flagship dry Riesling, sourced from old vines on Pfersigberg around Eguisheim and labelled Alsace AOC. Steely, mineral, built for a 20-year cellar.

wines.name: Leon Beyer Cuvee des Comtes d Eguisheim Gewurztraminer
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Beyer's flagship dry Gewurztraminer from Pfersigberg-area plots. Rose, lychee and white pepper, but unusually dry and structured for the grape.

pairings.why: Cumin-laced washed-rind cheese sings with Gewurztraminer's ginger and floral lift.
Roasted foie gras handles Gewurztraminer richness without sweetness.
Floral and spice register works with chilli, lemongrass and coconut.

history.summary: Beyer's Cuvee des Comtes d Eguisheim Gewurztraminer pairs the family's Eguisheim-Husseren old plots with a deliberately dry, structured style that runs counter to the off-dry house norm for the grape.

milestones.event: First Comtes d Eguisheim Gewurztraminer bottling
Wine becomes a fixture on three-star French restaurant lists for its dry register

wines.description: Beyer's flagship dry Gewurztraminer from Eguisheim old plots. Rose, lychee and ginger, but bone-dry and structured; the textbook Alsace dry Gewurz.

wines.name: Leon Beyer Cuvee Particuliere Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Beyer's mid-tier dry Riesling from Eguisheim plots, more accessible in price than the Comtes but in the same austere mineral idiom.

pairings.why: Brown-butter freshwater fish pairs with Riesling stone.
Salt and acid; classic Alsace bar pairing.
Cream-bacon flatbread cut by Riesling acid.

history.summary: The Cuvee Particuliere tier was created as a step below the Comtes d Eguisheim flagships, sourcing from younger Eguisheim and Husseren vines while keeping the bone-dry house style.

milestones.event: Cuvee Particuliere tier introduced

wines.description: Beyer's mid-tier dry Riesling from younger Eguisheim plots. Bone-dry stone and citrus; the accessible step below the Comtes d Eguisheim flagship.

wines.name: Leon Beyer Pinot Gris Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; no oak

taste.summary: Beyer's dry Pinot Gris reserve cuvee, fermented to dryness rather than the off-dry house style favoured by some Alsace producers. Smoky pear and almond weight.

pairings.why: The wine's smoky weight handles the pork and brine of Alsace kraut.
Pear and smoke ride a herbed roast bird beautifully.
Forest-floor and almond echo a mushroom tart's earthy register.

history.summary: Pinot Gris Reserve has long anchored Beyer's mid-range white selection, made bone-dry and unoaked, in a house style that explicitly rejects the off-dry Pinot Gris norm.

milestones.event: Pinot Gris Reserve becomes a fixture of the Beyer range

wines.description: Beyer's dry Pinot Gris reserve, fermented bone-dry against the off-dry Alsace norm. Smoky pear, almond and a salty finish that pairs choucroute beautifully.

wines.name: Schlumberger Kitterle Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schlumberger's flagship Kitterle Riesling from terraced sandstone vineyards above Guebwiller, often planted on slopes that need monorail access. Smoky, dry, structured.

pairings.why: Smoky Kitterle Riesling sits perfectly with smoked freshwater fish.
Riesling acid cuts pork-and-kraut while the Kitterle smoke layers in.
Crystalline cheese against the smoky stone register.

history.summary: Schlumberger farms more of Kitterle Grand Cru than any other house. The terraced sandstone slopes above Guebwiller are some of the steepest in Alsace, partly worked by monorail.

milestones.event: Kitterle named in the first Alsace Grand Cru decree
First Schlumberger Kitterle Riesling Grand Cru bottling
Domaine begins biodynamic conversion on selected Kitterle parcels

wines.description: Schlumberger's flagship Kitterle Riesling from steep sandstone terraces above Guebwiller. Smoky stone, lime and structured dryness; a benchmark Grand Cru Riesling.

wines.name: Schlumberger Kessler Gewurztraminer
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schlumberger's Kessler Gewurztraminer from sandstone slopes above Guebwiller, slightly off-dry in style. Rose, lychee and a ginger-spice register.

pairings.why: Aromatic Gewurz with Vosges washed-rind cheese is a regional classic.
Honeyed exotic fruit complements duck-liver richness.
Aromatic and gently sweet wine handles peanut, lime and chilli.

history.summary: Kessler is one of Schlumberger's flagship Grand Cru holdings, planted to Gewurztraminer on sandstone slopes above Guebwiller and bottled as a single-cru cuvee since the mid 1980s.

milestones.event: Kessler named in the first Alsace Grand Cru decree
First Schlumberger Kessler Gewurztraminer Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Schlumberger's Kessler Gewurztraminer from sandstone slopes above Guebwiller. Rose, lychee and ginger in the off-dry house style; a benchmark Grand Cru Gewurz.

wines.name: Schlumberger Saering Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schlumberger's most approachable Grand Cru Riesling, from sandstone slopes lower than Kitterle. Brisk citrus and stone.

pairings.why: Saline stone meets briny shellfish.
Bright citrus framework supports a delicate fish.
Riesling acid cuts pork and brine in the Alsace classic.

history.summary: Saering, an old word meaning 'pure water' in Alsatian, gave its name to one of the first Grand Crus, named in the 1975 decree. Schlumberger has bottled it as a single-cru cuvee since 1976.

milestones.event: Saering recognised in the first Alsace Grand Cru decree
First Schlumberger Saering Riesling Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Schlumberger's most approachable Grand Cru Riesling, from sandstone slopes around Guebwiller. Brisk lime, stone and the saline crispness of the cru.

wines.name: Schlumberger Spiegel Pinot Gris
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schlumberger's Spiegel Pinot Gris from sandstone-marl slopes above Guebwiller. Smoky pear and almond in an off-dry register.

pairings.why: Smoky pear and gentle sweetness against duck liver.
Fruit register meets the dish directly.
Earthy mushroom and the wine's almond-pear register echo.

history.summary: Spiegel sits below Kessler on the Guebwiller slopes and is shared between several producers. Schlumberger has bottled their portion as a Grand Cru Pinot Gris since 1985.

milestones.event: Spiegel recognised in the first Alsace Grand Cru decree
First Schlumberger Spiegel Pinot Gris bottling

wines.description: Schlumberger's Spiegel Pinot Gris from sandstone-marl slopes above Guebwiller. Smoky pear, almond and gentle off-dry texture; a versatile Grand Cru white.

wines.name: Schlumberger Cuvee Christine Vendanges Tardives
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Cuvee Christine is Schlumberger's family-named Vendanges Tardives Gewurztraminer, sourced from Kessler and Kitterle parcels. Honeyed and floral, made only in warm vintages.

pairings.why: Honeyed Gewurz drapes the duck-liver classic.
Cumin-spiced washed-rind meets the wine's ginger and rose.
Yellow plum tart slides under the wine's apricot register.

history.summary: Cuvee Christine is named for a Schlumberger family member and was conceived as a Vendanges Tardives Gewurztraminer in the heat-driven late 1980s. It is produced only in warm vintages.

milestones.event: First Cuvee Christine Vendanges Tardives bottling
Heat-vintage Christine becomes a benchmark of warm-year Alsace dessert wine

wines.description: Schlumberger's Vendanges Tardives Gewurztraminer named for a family member, sourced from Kessler and Kitterle slopes above Guebwiller in warm vintages only.

wines.name: Weinbach Schlossberg Riesling Cuvee Sainte Catherine
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Weinbach's flagship dry Riesling from old vines on Schlossberg granite, picked late on Saint Catherine's Day (25 November). The estate's most precise Riesling.

pairings.why: Cream and crayfish carry the wine's stone and citrus.
Saline drive matches the briny shellfish plate.
Crystalline cheese matches the granite and saline finish.

history.summary: Schlossberg, France's first Grand Cru, sits above the Weinbach domaine in Kientzheim. Weinbach's Cuvee Sainte Catherine bottling is picked late on Saint Catherine's Day from the oldest vines.

milestones.event: Schlossberg becomes France's first delimited Grand Cru
First Cuvee Sainte Catherine Riesling bottling at Weinbach
Domaine begins biodynamic conversion

wines.description: Weinbach's flagship dry Riesling from old vines on Schlossberg granite, picked late on Saint Catherine's Day. The estate's most precise Riesling.

wines.name: Weinbach Schlossberg Riesling L Inedit
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Weinbach's top Schlossberg parcel selection from the very oldest Schlossberg vines (planted in the 1940s). Released only in vintages that yield exceptional ripeness with retained acidity.

pairings.why: Top flat-fish slips with the wine's saline citrus and stone.
Sweet shellfish meets the wine's racy citrus.
Crystalline aged cheese against the granite and smoke.

history.summary: L Inedit (the unedited) was first bottled in 2004 from a 0.5 hectare parcel of 1940s-planted Schlossberg Riesling vines. The cuvee is made only in vintages that allow exceptional concentration with retained acidity.

milestones.event: First L Inedit Riesling bottling
Domaine certified biodynamic (Demeter)

wines.description: Weinbach's top Schlossberg parcel selection from 1940s-planted vines. Salt, smoke, citrus and racy acid; made only in exceptional vintages.

wines.name: Weinbach Furstentum Gewurztraminer Cuvee Laurence
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Weinbach's flagship Gewurztraminer from old vines on Furstentum's limestone slopes, named for one of Colette Faller's daughters. Concentrated, aromatic, structured.

pairings.why: Pungent washed-rind sings with Gewurz floral and ginger.
Concentrated wine handles the rich game bird and stone-fruit chutney.
Floral, exotic, gently sweet wine works with peanut, lime and chilli.

history.summary: Cuvee Laurence is Weinbach's selection from old Furstentum vines, named for Laurence Faller, daughter of the late Colette Faller and one of the architects of the modern estate.

milestones.event: First Cuvee Laurence Gewurztraminer bottling
Laurence Faller dies; her sister Catherine takes over winemaking with their mother Colette

wines.description: Weinbach's flagship Gewurztraminer from old vines on Furstentum limestone, named for Laurence Faller. Concentrated rose, lychee and ginger in the off-dry register.

wines.name: Weinbach Riesling Cuvee Theo
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Cuvee Theo, named for Theo Faller, is Weinbach's Alsace AOC Riesling, sourced from the Clos des Capucins vineyards around the domaine in Kientzheim.

pairings.why: Brown-butter fish with the Cuvee Theo's stone and lemon.
Riesling acid against pork and kraut.
Saline acid lifts the bivalve.

history.summary: Cuvee Theo is named for Theo Faller, who took over the domaine in 1979 and led its first wave of expansion. It sources from the walled Clos des Capucins around the cellar in Kientzheim.

milestones.event: Theo Faller takes over the domaine; Cuvee Theo introduced
Theo Faller dies prematurely; Colette Faller leads the domaine

wines.description: Weinbach's Alsace AOC Riesling Cuvee Theo, from the walled Clos des Capucins in Kientzheim. Stone, citrus and the granite drive of the estate's family vineyards.

wines.name: Weinbach Pinot Blanc Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Weinbach's Pinot Blanc, dominated by Auxerrois, from the walled Clos des Capucins vineyards. Soft, gentle, dinner-table friendly.

pairings.why: Creamy savoury tart with the wine's gentle apple and almond.
Cream-and-bacon flatbread with a softer Riesling-adjacent profile.
Gentle white that handles herbed weeknight bird.

history.summary: Weinbach's Pinot Blanc Reserve has been a steady fixture of the domaine since the mid 1980s, blending the two Pinot Blanc grapes (Pinot Blanc proper and Auxerrois) from the Clos des Capucins.

milestones.event: Pinot Blanc Reserve becomes a fixture of the domaine range

wines.description: Weinbach's Pinot Blanc Reserve from the walled Clos des Capucins, dominated by Auxerrois. Gentle apple, pear and almond; a dinner-table workhorse.

wines.name: Weinbach Altenbourg Pinot Gris Quintessence SGN
wines.classification: Alsace AOC Selection de Grains Nobles
wines.oak_regime: Old foudre; long lees ageing

taste.summary: Weinbach's noble-rot Pinot Gris from the Altenbourg lieu-dit. Honeycomb, candied apricot and saffron honey; a multi-decade dessert wine made only in exceptional vintages.

pairings.why: Honeyed unctuousness against the duck-liver classic.
Sweet wine balances the salty blue.
Yellow plum tart with the wine's saffron honey.

history.summary: Quintessence is Weinbach's top SGN designation, made from a strict selection of fully botrytised berries. The Altenbourg Pinot Gris Quintessence is produced only in exceptional vintages.

milestones.event: First Quintessence SGN bottling at Weinbach
Heat-vintage Quintessence becomes a benchmark of Alsace SGN

wines.description: Weinbach's noble-rot Pinot Gris from Altenbourg, top of the SGN pyramid. Saffron honey, candied apricot and marzipan; a multi-decade dessert wine.

wines.name: Zind Humbrecht Clos Saint Urbain Rangen Riesling
wines.classification: Alsace Grand Cru Rangen de Thann
wines.oak_regime: Long ferment, large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Zind-Humbrecht's flagship Riesling from the Clos Saint Urbain inside Grand Cru Rangen, the volcanic-slope masterpiece. Smoky, intensely mineral, structured for two decades.

pairings.why: Smoky volcanic mineral against caramelised umami.
Saline lift for a seafood platter.
Crystalline cheese against the volcanic stone.

history.summary: Rangen is the southernmost Grand Cru and the only Alsace cru on volcanic soils. Zind-Humbrecht acquired the walled Clos Saint Urbain in 1977 and made its first Rangen Riesling Grand Cru bottling in 1983.

milestones.event: Zind-Humbrecht acquires the Clos Saint Urbain
First Clos Saint Urbain Rangen Riesling Grand Cru bottling
Domaine fully biodynamic (Biodyvin)

wines.description: Zind-Humbrecht's flagship Riesling from the walled Clos Saint Urbain inside volcanic Grand Cru Rangen. Smoky, mineral, structured; an Alsace flagship dry Riesling.

wines.name: Zind Humbrecht Clos Saint Urbain Rangen Pinot Gris
wines.classification: Alsace Grand Cru Rangen de Thann
wines.oak_regime: Long ferment, large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Zind-Humbrecht's Clos Saint Urbain Rangen Pinot Gris, full-bodied and lightly off-dry. Honeyed pear, smoke and volcanic mineral grip.

pairings.why: Smoke and honeyed fruit with the dish's sweet richness.
Volcanic mineral cuts through, gentle sweetness cradles.
Earthy fungi and the wine's smoky mineral.

history.summary: The Clos Saint Urbain Pinot Gris from Rangen has been a foundational Zind-Humbrecht cuvee since 1983, born of the same 1977 acquisition that brought the family the volcanic walled vineyard.

milestones.event: First Clos Saint Urbain Rangen Pinot Gris Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Zind-Humbrecht's volcanic Pinot Gris from the walled Clos Saint Urbain in Grand Cru Rangen. Honeyed pear, smoke and granite-mineral grip; lightly off-dry.

wines.name: Zind Humbrecht Clos Hauserer Riesling

Domaine certified Demeter biodynamic

wines.description: Andre Ostertag's flagship Riesling from volcanic-sandstone Muenchberg Grand Cru above Nothalten. Sharp, smoky and structured; a Bas-Rhin masterpiece.

wines.name: Ostertag Fronholz Pinot Gris
wines.oak_regime: Old French oak barriques; no new oak

taste.summary: Andre Ostertag's Fronholz Pinot Gris from a sandstone hill above Epfig. Barrel-aged in old wood (unusual in Alsace) for a deliberately Burgundian-textured Pinot Gris.

pairings.why: Smoky pear for herbed roast pork.
Mushroom and bird against the wine's smoky almond.
Vosges washed-rind cheese for the wine's pear weight.

history.summary: Fronholz is Andre Ostertag's most personal lieu-dit, a sandstone hill above Epfig farmed since the 1980s and barrel-aged in old French oak, in a deliberately Burgundian-textured approach to Pinot Gris.

milestones.event: First Fronholz Pinot Gris bottling
Domaine certified Demeter biodynamic

wines.description: Andre Ostertag's barrel-aged Pinot Gris from Fronholz sandstone hill above Epfig. Biodynamic, Burgundian-textured, smoky pear and almond.

wines.name: Ostertag Heissenberg Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Andre Ostertag's Heissenberg Riesling from sandstone slopes near Nothalten, alongside Muenchberg. Pure, dry, biodynamic; the maison's everyday Bas-Rhin Riesling.

pairings.why: Saline citrus against the bivalve.
Riesling acid for pork-and-kraut.
Brown-butter fish for the wine's stone and lemon.

history.summary: Heissenberg is a sandstone slope adjacent to Muenchberg, farmed by the Ostertag family since the 1960s. Andre Ostertag's biodynamic Heissenberg Riesling has been bottled as a lieu-dit cuvee since 1985.

milestones.event: First Heissenberg Riesling lieu-dit bottling

wines.description: Andre Ostertag's biodynamic Heissenberg Riesling from sandstone slopes near Nothalten. Pure, dry, racy; the maison's everyday Bas-Rhin lieu-dit.

wines.name: Ostertag Vignoble d E Sylvaner
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old foudre

taste.summary: Andre Ostertag's Sylvaner from old vines around Epfig. Andre led the Alsace revival of Sylvaner as a serious grape rather than a sub-varietal afterthought.

pairings.why: Sylvaner's herb and salt against the cream and bacon.
Brisk Sylvaner for a board of cured meats.
Cream-bacon flatbread with bright Sylvaner.

history.summary: Andre Ostertag was a leading voice in the Alsace revival of Sylvaner as a serious grape rather than a sub-varietal afterthought. His Vignoble d E Sylvaner is from old vines around Epfig.

milestones.event: First Vignoble d E Sylvaner bottling
Sylvaner gets its own Grand Cru status on Zotzenberg, partly through Ostertag's advocacy

wines.description: Andre Ostertag's biodynamic Sylvaner from old vines around Epfig. A serious take on Alsace's understated grape; herb, salt and bright acid.

wines.name: Schoffit Clos Saint Theobald Rangen Riesling
wines.classification: Alsace Grand Cru Rangen de Thann
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schoffit's flagship Riesling from the Clos Saint Theobald inside Grand Cru Rangen, the southernmost Alsace volcano. Smoky and intensely mineral, structured for decades.

pairings.why: Saline volcanic Riesling against shellfish platter.
Caramelised umami against the wine's smoky mineral.
Crystalline cheese for the wine's volcanic stone.

history.summary: Clos Saint Theobald is one of the walled vineyards on Rangen, the southernmost Alsace Grand Cru and the only one on volcanic soil. Schoffit shares the slope with Zind-Humbrecht and has bottled the Riesling as a Grand Cru cuvee since 1984.

milestones.event: Rangen recognised in the Alsace Grand Cru decree
First Schoffit Clos Saint Theobald Riesling bottling

wines.description: Schoffit's flagship Riesling from the walled Clos Saint Theobald inside volcanic Grand Cru Rangen. Smoky, intensely mineral, multi-decade ageworthy.

wines.name: Schoffit Clos Saint Theobald Rangen Pinot Gris
wines.classification: Alsace Grand Cru Rangen de Thann
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schoffit's volcanic Pinot Gris from Clos Saint Theobald. Concentrated, smoky, lightly off-dry; a distinctive Alsace Pinot Gris expression.

pairings.why: Smoke and honeyed pear cushion the duck-liver classic.
Sweet-dark dish meets volcanic-mineral Pinot Gris.
Vosges washed-rind for the wine's exotic spice.

history.summary: Schoffit's Clos Saint Theobald Pinot Gris is one of the maison's flagship cuvees, sourced from the walled volcanic vineyard on Rangen and bottled since the early 1980s.

milestones.event: First Schoffit Clos Saint Theobald Pinot Gris bottling

wines.description: Schoffit's volcanic Pinot Gris from the walled Clos Saint Theobald inside Grand Cru Rangen. Smoky, honeyed pear and lightly off-dry; a Schoffit flagship.

wines.name: Schoffit Clos Saint Theobald Rangen Pinot Auxerrois
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schoffit's Auxerrois from Rangen volcanic soil; one of the few Alsace producers who bottles single-cru Auxerrois rather than blending it as Pinot Blanc.

pairings.why: Gentle smoky white for a herbed roast bird.
Cream-bacon flatbread for the wine's almond and peach.
Vosges washed-rind cheese against the wine's volcanic stone.

history.summary: Schoffit's Pinot Auxerrois on Rangen is a single-grape rarity, given that Auxerrois is usually blended into generic Pinot Blanc bottlings across Alsace.

milestones.event: First Schoffit Auxerrois Rangen lieu-dit bottling

wines.description: Schoffit's rare single-vineyard Pinot Auxerrois from Rangen volcanic soil. Smoky almond and peach; among the few standalone Alsace Auxerrois bottlings.

wines.name: Schoffit Harth Vieilles Vignes Pinot Gris
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Schoffit's old-vine Pinot Gris from the Harth plot near Colmar. A more affordable, plump expression of the Schoffit style, off-dry and broad-shouldered.

pairings.why: Plump off-dry Pinot Gris for the kraut and pork.
Honeyed pear and gentle sweetness against a tangy bird.
Yellow plum tart against the wine's stone-fruit register.

history.summary: The Harth Vieilles Vignes Pinot Gris comes from old vines on the gravelly Harth plot near Colmar and has been a fixture of the Schoffit range since 1990.

milestones.event: Harth Vieilles Vignes Pinot Gris becomes a fixture of the range

wines.description: Schoffit's old-vine Pinot Gris from the Harth plot near Colmar. Plump, off-dry, honeyed pear and almond; an accessible Schoffit cuvee.

wines.name: Dirler Cade Kessler Gewurztraminer
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Dirler-Cade's Kessler Gewurztraminer from sandstone slopes above Guebwiller, biodynamic since the early 1990s. Concentrated rose and lychee with a saline lift.

pairings.why: Aromatic Gewurz for Vosges washed-rind.
Honeyed wine cushions duck liver.
Floral exotic wine for chilli and coconut.

history.summary: Dirler-Cade is the Cade-side neighbour of Schlumberger on Kessler. They have farmed the parcel biodynamically since the early 1990s and bottled it as a single-cru Gewurztraminer since 1998.

milestones.event: First Dirler-Cade Kessler Gewurztraminer Grand Cru bottling
Estate certified Demeter biodynamic

wines.description: Dirler-Cade's biodynamic Kessler Gewurztraminer from sandstone slopes above Guebwiller. Concentrated rose, lychee and ginger with the cuvee's saline lift.

wines.name: Dirler Cade Saering Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Dirler-Cade's Saering Riesling from sandstone slopes around Guebwiller. Biodynamic and bone-dry; the maison's everyday Grand Cru.

pairings.why: Saline citrus against the bivalve.
Brown-butter fish for the wine's lemon and stone.
Riesling acid for pork-and-kraut.

history.summary: Saering, whose name means 'pure water,' is an approachable Grand Cru on the Guebwiller slopes. Dirler-Cade has bottled it as a single-cru cuvee biodynamically since 1998.

milestones.event: First Dirler-Cade Saering Riesling Grand Cru bottling
Estate certified Demeter biodynamic

wines.description: Dirler-Cade's biodynamic Saering Riesling from sandstone slopes around Guebwiller. Bone-dry, lime, stone; the maison's everyday Grand Cru.

wines.name: Dirler Cade Kitterle Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Dirler-Cade's Kitterle Riesling from terraced sandstone slopes above Guebwiller. Smoky, dry, structured; biodynamic since the mid 2000s.

pairings.why: Smoky Kitterle Riesling for smoked freshwater fish.
Riesling acid for pork-and-kraut with the cuvee's smoke layered in.
Crystalline cheese against the wine's smoky stone register.

history.summary: Dirler-Cade farms a parcel on the terraced Kitterle slopes alongside Schlumberger and has bottled it as a single-cru cuvee biodynamically since 1998.

milestones.event: First Dirler-Cade Kitterle Riesling Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Dirler-Cade's biodynamic Kitterle Riesling from steep sandstone terraces above Guebwiller. Smoky stone, lime and structured dryness from a small biodynamic farmer.

wines.name: Dirler Cade Spiegel Gewurztraminer
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Dirler-Cade's Spiegel Gewurztraminer from sandstone-marl slopes above Bergholtz. Concentrated rose and lychee with a saline lift.

pairings.why: Aromatic Gewurz for Vosges washed-rind.
Concentrated honey-floral wine for duck liver.
Floral exotic wine for peanut, lime and chilli.

history.summary: Spiegel is shared between several producers; Dirler-Cade's parcel produces a Gewurztraminer they have bottled as a single-cru since 2000, biodynamic since the mid 2000s.

milestones.event: First Dirler-Cade Spiegel Gewurztraminer Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Dirler-Cade's biodynamic Spiegel Gewurztraminer from sandstone-marl slopes above Bergholtz. Concentrated rose, lychee and ginger with a saline lift.

wines.name: Frick Steinert Pinot Blanc Zero Sulfite
wines.oak_regime: Old foudre; long lees ageing

taste.summary: Pierre Frick's Pinot Blanc from the limestone Steinert vineyard, bottled with zero added sulfites. Pioneering natural-wine work in Alsace from 1999 onward.

pairings.why: Creamy savoury tart for the wine's salt and almond.
Cream-bacon flatbread with bright natural wine.
Natural Pinot Blanc handles a board of cured meats.

history.summary: Pierre Frick has been a pioneer of zero-sulfite winemaking in Alsace, bottling no-added-sulfite cuvees since 1999. The Steinert Pinot Blanc is one of his signature zero-sulfite wines.

milestones.event: Frick converts to organic farming
First Frick zero-sulfite cuvees

wines.description: Pierre Frick's zero-sulfite Pinot Blanc from limestone Steinert. Biodynamic and natural; a pioneering Alsace no-added-sulfite cuvee.

wines.name: Frick Steinert Pinot Gris Macere
wines.oak_regime: Skin-contact maceration; old foudre ageing; no new oak

taste.summary: Pierre Frick's skin-macerated Pinot Gris from the Steinert vineyard. Orange wine in the textbook Alsace register: dried apricot, citrus peel, a modest tannic grip.

pairings.why: Pungent washed-rind cheese against the wine's tannic grip and dried fruit.
Orange wine tannin supports a long-cooked meat.
Skin-contact wine handles cumin, coriander and chilli.

history.summary: Frick has been an early Alsace producer of skin-macerated white wine (orange wine), with the Steinert Pinot Gris Macere a regular bottling since the late 2000s.

milestones.event: First Frick skin-macerated Pinot Gris bottling

wines.description: Pierre Frick's biodynamic skin-macerated Pinot Gris from Steinert. Dried apricot, orange peel, smoky tea; an early Alsace orange wine.

wines.name: Christian Binner Saveurs Printanieres
wines.oak_regime: Skin contact; old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Binner's spring-bottled skin-contact field blend from Pinot family grapes around Ammerschwihr. Light, fragrant orange wine for casual drinking.

pairings.why: Cream-bacon flatbread for a soft skin-contact white.
Vosges washed-rind for the wine's gentle tannic grip.
Aromatic skin-contact field-blend for lemongrass and chilli.

history.summary: Christian Binner is a flag-bearer of natural winemaking in Alsace. Saveurs Printanieres (spring flavours) is his entry skin-contact cuvee, a field-blend of Pinot-family grapes bottled the spring after harvest.

milestones.event: Binner converts the estate to biodynamic farming
Saveurs Printanieres bottling becomes a fixture

wines.description: Christian Binner's spring-bottled skin-contact field blend of Pinot-family grapes. Biodynamic and natural; a light, fragrant entry orange wine.

wines.name: Christian Binner Schlossberg Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Old foudre; long lees ageing; no new oak

taste.summary: Binner's natural-style Schlossberg Riesling. Bone-dry, granite-driven, often with a slight reductive twang from the no-added-sulfite winemaking.

pairings.why: Cream-sauced freshwater fish for the wine's granite mineral.
Saline lemon against the briny bivalve.
Crystalline cheese against the wine's racy stone.

history.summary: Binner's Schlossberg Riesling has been bottled as a Grand Cru cuvee since 1998 and is a benchmark of the natural-wine movement applied to top Alsace terroir.

milestones.event: First Binner Schlossberg Riesling Grand Cru bottling
Estate fully biodynamic

wines.description: Binner's biodynamic Schlossberg Riesling, naturally vinified. Bone-dry, granite-driven, structured; the natural-wine register applied to Alsace Grand Cru.

wines.name: Christian Binner Kaefferkopf Cuvee Beatrice
wines.oak_regime: Old foudre; long lees ageing; no new oak

taste.summary: Binner's complanted Kaefferkopf, named for his daughter Beatrice. Field-blend of noble grapes in a natural-wine register; Kaefferkopf became Alsace's 51st Grand Cru in 2007.

pairings.why: Honeyed complanted wine for duck liver classic.
Aromatic complanted wine for Vosges washed-rind.
Concentrated complanted white for game bird.

history.summary: Kaefferkopf became Alsace's 51st Grand Cru in 2007 and is unique in officially permitting complanted blends. Binner's Cuvee Beatrice was among the first complanted bottlings under the new Kaefferkopf decree.

milestones.event: Kaefferkopf becomes Alsace's 51st Grand Cru, permitting complantation
First Binner Kaefferkopf Cuvee Beatrice bottling

wines.description: Binner's complanted Kaefferkopf Grand Cru, named for his daughter Beatrice. Biodynamic, natural and one of the first complanted bottlings under the 2007 decree.

wines.name: Barmes Buecher Cremant d Alsace Extra Brut
wines.oak_regime: Tirage on lees 30 to 60 months; some lots aged in old foudre before tirage

taste.summary: Barmes-Buecher's vintage-dated extra-brut Cremant. Long-aged on lees in a Champagne-style Alsace Cremant, biodynamic, often zero-dosage.

pairings.why: Bone-dry traditional-method bubbles for raw shellfish.
Long-lees biscuity Cremant for canapes.
Salt and brioche aperitif pairing.

history.summary: Barmes-Buecher's vintage-dated extra brut Cremant is a serious traditional-method bottling in Alsace, with long lees ageing and biodynamic farming.

milestones.event: Cremant d Alsace AOC created
First Barmes-Buecher vintage-dated extra brut Cremant

wines.description: Barmes-Buecher's biodynamic vintage-dated extra-brut Cremant d Alsace. Long-aged on lees, often zero-dosage; one of the region's serious sparkling cuvees.

wines.name: Barmes Buecher Hengst Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Barmes-Buecher's Hengst Riesling from calcareous-marl slopes above Wintzenheim. Biodynamic, limestone-driven, dry and slowly developing.

pairings.why: Limestone Riesling against shellfish platter.
Cream-sauced freshwater fish for racy stone.
Crystalline cheese against the wine's saline mineral.

history.summary: Barmes-Buecher's Hengst Riesling has been a single-cru cuvee since the mid 1990s, biodynamic since 2001 (Demeter).

milestones.event: First Barmes-Buecher Hengst Riesling Grand Cru bottling
Estate begins biodynamic conversion

wines.description: Barmes-Buecher's biodynamic Hengst Riesling from calcareous-marl above Wintzenheim. Limestone, salt and the slow-development of a serious Grand Cru.

wines.name: Barmes Buecher Steingrubler Gewurztraminer
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Barmes-Buecher's Steingrubler Gewurztraminer from limestone slopes above Wettolsheim. Concentrated rose and lychee in a saline biodynamic register.

pairings.why: Aromatic Gewurz for Vosges washed-rind.
Honeyed wine cushions duck liver.
Floral exotic wine for chilli and coconut.

history.summary: Barmes-Buecher's Steingrubler Gewurztraminer has been a Grand Cru cuvee since 1998, biodynamic since 2001.

milestones.event: First Barmes-Buecher Steingrubler Gewurztraminer Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Barmes-Buecher's biodynamic Gewurztraminer from limestone slopes above Wettolsheim. Concentrated rose, lychee and ginger with a saline biodynamic register.

wines.name: Rene Mure Clos Saint Landelin Vorbourg Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Mure's Clos Saint Landelin is a monopole inside the Vorbourg Grand Cru, south of Colmar. The Riesling is concentrated, saline, structured by the cuvee's limestone-marl.

pairings.why: Cream-sauced freshwater fish for limestone Riesling.
Saline Riesling against shellfish platter.
Crystalline cheese against the wine's limestone register.

history.summary: Clos Saint Landelin is a 21 hectare monopole inside the Vorbourg Grand Cru south of Rouffach, farmed by the Mure family since 1935. The Riesling has been bottled as a Grand Cru cuvee since 1985.

milestones.event: Mure family acquires the Clos Saint Landelin
Vorbourg recognised in the Alsace Grand Cru decree
First Clos Saint Landelin Riesling Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Mure's Riesling from the Clos Saint Landelin monopole inside Vorbourg Grand Cru. Saline limestone, structured and reliably ageworthy.

wines.name: Rene Mure Clos Saint Landelin Vorbourg Pinot Noir V
wines.oak_regime: 16 to 18 months in French oak, modest new-oak share

taste.summary: Mure's serious Pinot Noir from the Clos Saint Landelin monopole on Vorbourg. The V is the cuvee bottled when Vorbourg-grown Pinot Noir warranted a Grand-Cru-quality cuvee, though officially labelled Alsace AOC.

pairings.why: Cherry-and-graphite Pinot for the game bird.
Pinot Noir register meets the creamy Alsace chicken.
Pinot Noir earth softens the pungent washed-rind.

history.summary: Mure's V cuvee was created to bottle the top-tier Pinot Noir selection from the Clos Saint Landelin monopole. Since the 2022 decree, Vorbourg has been included in the Grand Crus open to Pinot Noir Grand Cru classification.

milestones.event: First V cuvee Pinot Noir bottling
Vorbourg becomes a Grand Cru open to Pinot Noir under new Alsace decree

wines.description: Mure's serious Pinot Noir from the Clos Saint Landelin monopole on Vorbourg. Burgundian-register cherry and graphite from south Alsace's red pioneer.

wines.name: Rene Mure Cote de Rouffach Pinot Gris
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old foudre

taste.summary: Mure's village-level Pinot Gris from the Rouffach hillsides. Plump, lightly off-dry, honeyed pear in a friendly register.

pairings.why: Plump Pinot Gris for the kraut and pork.
Honeyed pear for the herbed roast.
Cream-bacon flatbread for the wine's gentle weight.

history.summary: Cote de Rouffach Pinot Gris is Mure's village-level expression of the grape, sourced from the rolling slopes around Rouffach in the southern Haut-Rhin.

milestones.event: Cote de Rouffach tier becomes a fixture of the Mure range

wines.description: Mure's village-level Pinot Gris from the Rouffach hillsides. Plump, lightly off-dry, honeyed pear; a friendly mid-range Alsace Pinot Gris.

wines.name: Rene Mure Cremant d Alsace Brut
wines.oak_regime: Tirage on lees 24 to 36 months

taste.summary: Mure's house Cremant brut, a Pinot-family blend with a splash of Riesling for lift. Reliable traditional-method Alsace bubbles at a friendly price.

pairings.why: Brisk traditional-method bubbles for any aperitif occasion.
Bone-dry bubbles for raw shellfish.
Salt and brioche; classic Alsace bar pairing.

history.summary: Mure's house Cremant d Alsace Brut has been a fixture of the maison since the early 1990s, with a Pinot-Blanc and Auxerrois base lifted by a small percentage of Riesling.

milestones.event: Cremant d Alsace AOC created
Mure house Cremant Brut becomes a fixture of the range

wines.description: Mure's house Cremant d Alsace Brut, Pinot-family blend with a Riesling lift. Reliable traditional-method Alsace bubbles at a friendly price.

wines.name: Kuentz Bas Eichberg Gewurztraminer
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Kuentz-Bas's flagship Gewurztraminer from Eichberg above Eguisheim. Concentrated rose and lychee with the cuvee's saline mineral lift; biodynamic since 2007.

pairings.why: Aromatic Gewurz for Vosges washed-rind classic.
Honeyed Gewurz cushions duck liver.
Aromatic exotic register meets star anise and cinnamon.

history.summary: Kuentz-Bas has been bottling Eichberg Gewurztraminer as a single-cru cuvee since the mid 1980s. The estate began biodynamic conversion in 2007 under new owners Jean-Baptiste and Antoine Kuentz.

milestones.event: First Kuentz-Bas Eichberg Gewurztraminer Grand Cru bottling
New ownership begins biodynamic conversion

wines.description: Kuentz-Bas's biodynamic flagship Gewurztraminer from Eichberg Grand Cru above Eguisheim. Concentrated rose, lychee and ginger with a saline mineral lift.

wines.name: Kuentz Bas Pfersigberg Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; no new oak

taste.summary: Kuentz-Bas's Pfersigberg Riesling from limestone slopes above Eguisheim. Dry, structured, saline; biodynamic in the new-ownership era.

pairings.why: Limestone Riesling against shellfish platter.
Riesling acid for pork-and-kraut.
Crystalline cheese for the wine's mineral cut.

history.summary: Kuentz-Bas farms a parcel of old Riesling vines on Pfersigberg above Eguisheim, on the warm limestone slopes that share their name with peach trees (pfersig). Biodynamic conversion since 2007.

milestones.event: First Kuentz-Bas Pfersigberg Riesling Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Kuentz-Bas's biodynamic Pfersigberg Riesling from limestone slopes above Eguisheim. Dry, saline, structured; a serious Grand Cru from a renewed maison.

wines.name: Kuentz Bas Trois Chateaux Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old foudre

taste.summary: Kuentz-Bas's Trois Chateaux Riesling, named for the three medieval castles above Husseren-les-Chateaux. The maison's village-level dry Riesling.

pairings.why: Riesling acid for pork-and-kraut.
Saline citrus against the bivalve.
Cream-bacon flatbread cut by bright acid.

history.summary: Trois Chateaux is Kuentz-Bas's village-level dry Riesling, named for the three medieval castles above Husseren-les-Chateaux. A reliable mid-range Alsace Riesling.

milestones.event: Trois Chateaux tier becomes a fixture of the range

wines.description: Kuentz-Bas's village-level dry Riesling, named for the three medieval castles above Husseren-les-Chateaux. Bright lime, apple and stone in a friendly format.

wines.name: Valentin Zusslin Clos Liebenberg Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; long lees ageing

taste.summary: Valentin Zusslin's Clos Liebenberg monopole Riesling, on south-facing limestone above Orschwihr. Biodynamic and structured, with the salinity of a hillside-walled vineyard.

pairings.why: Brown-butter fish for the wine's stone and lemon.
Limestone Riesling against shellfish platter.
Crystalline cheese against the wine's saline mineral.

history.summary: Clos Liebenberg is Zusslin's south-facing monopole walled vineyard above Orschwihr. Bottled as a single-vineyard cuvee since the mid 1990s, Demeter biodynamic since 1997.

milestones.event: Estate certified Demeter biodynamic
First Clos Liebenberg Riesling bottling

wines.description: Valentin Zusslin's biodynamic Clos Liebenberg monopole Riesling, south-facing limestone above Orschwihr. Saline, structured and slow-developing.

wines.name: Valentin Zusslin Pfingstberg Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; long lees ageing

taste.summary: Zusslin's Pfingstberg Grand Cru Riesling from sandstone-marl slopes around Orschwihr. Biodynamic, dry, structured for the cellar.

pairings.why: Shellfish concentration against racy Riesling.
Cream-sauced freshwater fish for the wine's stone.
Crystalline cheese against the wine's salt and stone.

history.summary: Pfingstberg above Orschwihr is one of the southern Alsace Grand Crus, named for the Pentecost flowers that bloom on its slopes. Zusslin has bottled it as a single-cru cuvee since 1990.

milestones.event: First Zusslin Pfingstberg Riesling Grand Cru bottling

wines.description: Valentin Zusslin's biodynamic Pfingstberg Grand Cru Riesling. Sandstone-marl slopes, dry, structured; one of the south Alsace Grand Crus.

wines.name: Valentin Zusslin Cremant d Alsace Rose
wines.oak_regime: Tirage on lees 36 to 48 months

taste.summary: Valentin Zusslin's biodynamic Cremant Rose, made from 100 percent Pinot Noir. Salmon-pink, long-lees ageing, more serious than a typical Cremant rose.

pairings.why: Pink bubbles with the lemon-cured fish.
Pinot-led rose against summer berries.
Cremant rose handles a cured-meat board.

history.summary: Zusslin's 100 percent Pinot Noir Cremant Rose has been a fixture since 2002, an ageworthy traditional-method rose in the Alsace context.

milestones.event: Cremant d Alsace AOC created
First Zusslin Pinot Noir Cremant Rose bottling

wines.description: Valentin Zusslin's biodynamic 100 percent Pinot Noir Cremant Rose. Salmon-pink, long-lees, serious; an ageworthy traditional-method Alsace rose.

wines.name: Leon Boesch Zinnkoepfle Riesling
wines.oak_regime: Large old foudre; long lees ageing

taste.summary: Leon Boesch's Zinnkoepfle Riesling from south-facing limestone-marl slopes above Westhalten. Biodynamic Demeter, dry and structured.

pairings.why: Limestone Riesling against shellfish.
Riesling acid for pork-and-kraut.
Crystalline cheese for the wine's salt and stone.

history.summary: Zinnkoepfle, the highest-altitude Grand Cru of Alsace at 420 metres, gives an austere, slow-developing Riesling. Leon Boesch has bottled it as a Grand Cru cuvee since 1995, Demeter biodynamic since 1998.

Estate certified Demeter biodynamic
